section.title: Installing PostgreSQL
section.p: 1) Using source code to compile PostgreSQL
Compile and install after de-compressing (Need root privileges)
section.codeblock: $> tar -zxvf postgresql-9.3.4.tar.gz $> cd postgresql-9.3.4/ $> ./configure && make && make install
section.p: 5) Add PostgreSQL's lib library in environment variables

note.p: We suggest that users put PostgreSQL'slib to environment variables of sdbadmin, or you may need to add PostgreSQL'slib manually into LD_LIBRARY_PATH every time you login as sdbadmin

section.p: 7) Initializing the data directory(Can only be operate one time!)

section.title: Install SequoiaDB-PostgreSQL plug-in
section.p: If the libdir directory is still not exist, you need to create the directory manually.
Create extension directory in the sharedir directory
3) Copy PostgreSQL's extension file in SequoiaDB's installing package
After SequoiaDB installed, copy sdb_fdw.so from directory postgresql to PostgreSQL'slib directory. SequoiaDB's default installing path is /opt/sequoiadb
4) Copy scripts of sdb_fdw.control and sdb_fdw-1.0.sql to directory extension, these two scripts need to be edited manually.
section.codeblock: # sdb_fdw extension comment = 'foreign data wrapper for SequoiaDB access' default_version = '1.0' module_pathname = '$libdir/sdb_fdw' relocatable = true

/* contrib/sdb_fdw/sdb_fdw--1.0.sql */ -- complain if script is sourced in psql, rather than via CREATE EXTENSION \echo Use "CREATE EXTENSION sdb_fdw" to load this file. \quit CREATE FUNCTION sdb_fdw_handler() RETURNS fdw_handler AS 'MODULE_PATHNAME' LANGUAGE C STRICT; CREATE FUNCTION sdb_fdw_validator(text[], oid) RETURNS void AS 'MODULE_PATHNAME' LANGUAGE C STRICT; CREATE FOREIGN DATA WRAPPER sdb_fdw HANDLER sdb_fdw_handler VALIDATOR sdb_fdw_validator;

section.title: Deploy PostgreSQL
section.p: 1) Check whether the port is occupied
PostgreSQL's default start port is '5432', check if the port is available (we suggest inspecting in root mode, and do the rest operations in sdbadmin still)
If port 5432 is occupied, or user want to change the port, please execute:
2) Start the PostgreSQL service process(must use sdbadmin to execute)
section.codeblock: tcp 0 0 127.0.0.1:5432 0.0.0.0:* LISTEN 20502/postgres unix 2 [ ACC ] STREAM LISTENING 40776754 20502/postgres /tmp/.s.PGSQL.5432
